sprint: World Builder — Overflow-Menu Duplicate Didn't Actually Leave View Mode
task: Fixed a real, user-reported bug: 'Builder: duplicate option is not working for official theme -- we should be able to duplicate it to use as a template for a new theme.' An Official World opens read-only (View Mode), and Duplicate is deliberately the one action left enabled -- it's documented as the intended way to leave View Mode. The Welcome-screen card's own Duplicate button already regenerated the copy's manifest id for exactly this reason (a plain ProjectStore.duplicate() keeps the source's id, which would make the copy match the Official-repository id check too and silently reopen in View Mode). But the in-Workspace overflow menu's 'Duplicate World' -- the one reachable from inside an open Official World, the actual reported flow -- called ProjectStore.duplicate() directly and never got that same fix, so duplicating from there produced a copy that opened right back into View Mode, reading as 'Duplicate doesn't work.' Fixed by extracting the id-regeneration logic into one shared _duplicateProject() helper used by both call sites. Verified via Playwright: both the Welcome-card button and the in-Workspace overflow-menu action now produce a Project with a fresh, distinct manifest id, confirmed independently for each. goldenBuild.js's full 30-assertion suite passes unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
